title: Nursery Rhyme. CCLXXIV. Gaffers And Gammers.
content:
There was an old woman of Norwich,
        Who lived upon nothing but porridge;
            Parading the town,
            She turned cloak into gown,
        This thrifty old woman of Norwich.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Unknown delivers a powerful performance in "Nursery Rhyme. CCLXXIV. Gaffers And Gammers."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
